title: Jenkins の 401 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Several factual claims regarding the expiration of Jenkins API tokens are incorrect; Jenkins does not natively expire API tokens, though it encourages rotation.

The article's suggestion to prefer 'Username with password' over 'Secret text' credentials for preventing console output is misleading, as both types are securely handled by the 'withCredentials' step in pipelines.
The claim about API token expiration settings being available in Jenkins 2.176+ is incorrect, contributing to potential user confusion or wasted effort in searching for a non-existent feature.

The article mentions checking 'jenkins.xml' for authentication settings in Docker environments, which is less direct than configuration via environment variables or mounted 'JENKINS_HOME' contents typically used in Docker.
freshness score is below the threshold
one or more claims are unsupported or unverifiable
required_actions: Correct all claims related to Jenkins API token expiration, stating that tokens do not natively expire.

Clarify the secure and appropriate use of 'Secret text' and 'Username with password' credential types in Jenkins pipelines, emphasizing that both can prevent console output when correctly implemented with 'withCredentials'.
Remove or revise the specific version (Jenkins 2.176+) associated with API token expiration settings.
Update the guidance for Docker container authentication settings to reflect common Docker practices (e.g., environment variables, mounted volumes for JENKINS_HOME).
Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.

sources.title: Jenkins api token expiry - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Jenkins API tokens do not have a time limit set for their expiry.

sources.title: Create an API Token in Jenkins that is Never Expiring
sources.claim: Jenkins API tokens do not expire; the recommendation to regenerate them every 6 months is a best practice, not an enforced expiration.

sources.title: Using credentials - Jenkins
sources.claim: Jenkins can store credentials as 'Secret text' (for API tokens) or 'Username and password', and credentials are stored in an encrypted form and handled via credential IDs to minimize exposure in Pipeline projects.

sources.title: Jenkins Secrets: Managing Credentials Without Compromising Security - Infisical
sources.claim: Jenkins supports 'Secret text' for API tokens and 'Username and password' for basic authentication, and credentials stored in Jenkins are encrypted and hidden from logs and console output when used in pipelines.

sources.title: Setting Up and Using Credentials in Jenkins with examples | by W Shamim - Medium
sources.claim: Jenkins Credentials include 'Username with Password' and 'Secret Text' types, both designed to securely store sensitive information and be used in pipelines.

sources.title: Managing Credentials In Jenkins | Octopus blog
sources.claim: Jenkins offers 'Secret text' and 'Usernames and passwords' as credential types, and when used correctly in pipelines, they can prevent exposure.

sources.title: Upgrading to Jenkins LTS 2.176.x
sources.claim: Jenkins 2.176 introduced improvements to CSRF protection, and API tokens have not required a CSRF token since Jenkins 2.96.

sources.title: Rest API authentication failure - Jenkins - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: A 401 error in Jenkins indicates an authentication problem.

sources.title: jenkinsをApache経由でBasic認証するときに401が出て困る問題の対処（ハマったメモ）
sources.claim: A 401 status code can be returned by Jenkins with an 'Bad credentials' exception.

sources.title: Jenkins errors and solutions
sources.claim: A 401 error with a message like 'Invalid JWT/S token' can occur if the API token is not provided correctly.

sources.title: 「Jenkinsにアクセスするには認証が必要です」エラーを解決！
sources.claim: Jenkins API tokens can become invalid or expired, leading to authentication errors.

sources.title: API Tokens get removed from Client controllers or Managed controllers
sources.claim: API tokens defined at the controller level can be wiped out when synchronized with an Operations Center, effectively invalidating them due to a security realm change or synchronization process.

sources.title: How to persist Jenkins API token? · Issue #454 · jenkinsci/kubernetes-operator - GitHub
sources.claim: API tokens can be invalidated after a Jenkins restart when user authentication is set up with LDAP.

sources.title: Jenkins User Management API Guide - Stitchflow
sources.claim: Deleting a user does not immediately invalidate existing API tokens, meaning an account can continue to authenticate until session expiry.

sources.title: Authentication in Jenkins does not work · Issue #1596 · jenkinsci/gitlab-plugin - GitHub
sources.claim: 401 errors frequently occur when using API tokens for automated access from pipeline scripts, such as with GitLab webhooks.

sources.title: Failed to connect to repository - 401 on Jenkins + Codebase Git - Server Fault
sources.claim: 401 errors can occur when Jenkins pipelines attempt to connect to repositories with incorrect authentication.

sources.title: API Token in Jenkins REST API - CloudBees
sources.claim: API token support was added for the Jenkins REST API to simplify authentication.

sources.title: Getting 401 unauthorized in my Jenkins pipeline script, but when I access it manually I am able to click the report with out any issues - SonarQube Server / Community Build
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized errors can occur in Jenkins pipeline scripts when accessing external services like SonarQube due to incorrect token configuration.

sources.title: Security Hardening: New API token system in Jenkins 2.129+
sources.claim: Jenkins encourages users to rotate API tokens by highlighting their creation date, and provides features to revoke tokens to enhance security.

sources.title: About Jenkins tokens - Guillaume Quéré
sources.claim: API token functionality changed significantly in Jenkins 2.129+, with newer tokens being stored as hashes and allowing multiple tokens per user.

sources.title: How to get the API token for Jenkins - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: Jenkins 2.129+ introduced changes to API token configuration, allowing multiple named tokens and individual revocation, whereas older versions had a single API token.

sources.title: Jenkins API token | GitGuardian documentation
sources.claim: Since version 2.129, Jenkins offers a new API token system for programmatic authentication.

sources.title: How to Configure Jenkins Security - OneUptime
sources.claim: Jenkins authentication verifies user identity, and multiple security realms are available; enabling security and properly configuring the security realm (e.g., in config.xml) is crucial.

sources.title: How to fix the Jenkins 401 Unauthorized when using java API and provided correct credentials - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: For HTTP Basic Authentication, the 'username:API_token' string is Base64 encoded.

sources.title: Ephemeral Jenkins Users + API Tokens using Hashicorp Vault - DEV Community
sources.claim: Using API tokens for machine-to-machine communication with Jenkins can avoid hitting identity provider API rate limits when a security realm like SAML/LDAP/Active Directory is used, suggesting that 401 issues can be common in these migration scenarios.

sources.title: Intermittently receiving 401 Unauthorized from api while using a access token (PAT)
sources.claim: 401 Unauthorized issues can occur with OpenId Connect Authentication (SSO) and Jenkins API tokens, especially if API tokens are tied to OIDC sessions.

unsupported_claims: Jenkinsのセキュリティ向上のため、APIトークンには有効期限が設定されることがあります。
認証情報を「シークレットテキスト」ではなく「ユーザー名とパスワード」タイプのCredentialsとして登録し、パイプラインから参照することで、トークンがコンソール出力に表示される問題を防げます。
Jenkins 2.176以降を使用している場合、APIトークンの有効期限設定が利用可能です。
